ports.banner: 220 VSFTPD 3.0.3 - Secure FTP Server connection active.
ports.description: Legacy plaintext transfer protocol. Vulnerable to credential sniffing and man-in-the-middle attacks.
ports.remediation: Disable anonymous FTP. Upgrade to SFTP (SSH File Transfer Protocol) using public key authentication.

ports.description: Secure shell transport channel. Exposes remote command execution interfaces.
ports.remediation: Disable password-based logins, require certificate keys, rate limit authentication attempts, and customize default port.

ports.banner: Telnetd Login Remote Console - Session Ready.
ports.description: Plaintext terminal protocol. Complete absence of session encryption triggers massive passive capture vulnerabilities.
ports.remediation: Immediately decommission Telnet service. Replace with SSH.

ports.description: Connection actively refused by host operating system (RST pack returned).
ports.remediation: No active mitigation required. Ensure default-deny holds valid.

ports.description: NoSQL In-memory caching gateway. Often exposed unauthenticated, allowing arbitrary file retrieval and remote code execution.
ports.remediation: Enable authentication (REQUIREPASS parameter), bind only to trusted interfaces, and configure TLS.

ports.description: Connection actively refused by host operating system (RST pack returned).
ports.remediation: No active mitigation required. Ensure default-deny holds valid.

ports.description: NoSQL document datastore. Public exposure might lead to unauthenticated database dumps or ransomware attacks.
ports.remediation: Configure internal IP binding, enforce robust SCRAM-SHA auth schemas, and use TLS bindings.
